mappings.rationaleAI: Personality psychology and abnormal psychology address definitions of personality (A). Self-actualization relates to self-awareness (C.1). Abnormal psychology connects to mental disorders (D).
Id, ego, superego and psychosexual development address psychoanalytic theories of personality (A.2). Sexual deviation relates to psychosexual disorders (D.1).
The unconscious and obsessional neurosis address psychoanalytic theories (A.2). Clinical evolution relates to mental disorders (D.1).
Collective unconscious and archetypes address psychoanalytic theories (A.2). Psychological types relate to eclectic theories (A.4).
Selfhood, self-identity, and self-knowledge directly address development of awareness of self (C.1).

Authenticity, freedom, and personal responsibility address development of self-awareness (C.1).
Assessment and diagnosis address personality assessment methods (B.1). Therapeutic approaches address coping responses (C.3). PTSD connects to mental disorders (D.1).

CBT, counselling, and mindfulness address coping responses to environmental strains (C.3).

Hallucinations, delusions, and pharmacological treatments address schizophrenia (D.1). Biological basis addresses causation (D.3).
Unipolar and bipolar disorder address affective disorders (D.1). Societal impact addresses prevalence (D.2). Antidepressants address causation (D.3).
Phobias, OCD, and PTSD address anxiety disorders (D.1). Epidemiology addresses prevalence (D.2). Amygdala and genetics address causation (D.3).
Historical responses to mental disturbance address persisting disturbances (D) and major mental disorders (D.1).
Autism spectrum disorder addresses disorders of infancy or childhood (D.1).
Psychopathy as personality disorder addresses personality disorders (D.1).
Substance dependence as strain addresses stresses on personality functioning (C.2) and substance use disorders (D.1).

Obedience and group behaviour address social analytic theories (A.3). Attitudes relate to eclectic theories (A.4).
Happiness and human flourishing address the subjective aspect of personality (C.1).
Epidemiological methods provide context for prevalence of mental disorders (D.2).
Physiological mechanisms provide biological context for physiological theories of personality (A.1).
Covers pharmacological mechanisms for treating mental illness and addiction — relevant to causation of mental disorders.
Covers hypothesis testing, experimental design, and measurement validity — directly relevant to evaluating assessment techniques (unresolved leaf B.2) through psychometric statistical frameworks.
